critic_raw:
IS and FID are computed using an Inception network pretrained on ImageNet. For counterfactual generation tasks outside the ImageNet domain (e.g., medical imaging, abstract patterns), these metrics measure Inception feature similarity rather than semantic quality of the counterfactual manipulation. Critically, IS and FID cannot verify whether counterfactual changes are semantically meaningful — a GAN can produce high-IS samples while generating counterfactuals that are visually indistinguishable from non-counterfactuals.

The discriminator is conditioned on 'counterfactual contexts sampled from rich prior knowledge sources.' The nature of these knowledge sources — whether they are fixed class attributes, learned embeddings, or structured prior knowledge — is not specified. If the prior is narrow or low-entropy, the generator can exploit the predictability of counterfactual contexts to produce high-quality samples without actually learning diverse causal interventions.

The independence of the counterfactual reasoning from standard GAN training is not empirically verified. A GAN can achieve high IS/FID scores by ignoring the counterfactual conditioning (a known mode-collapse variant) while mapping most of the signal to unconditional generation. If the counterfactual manipulation is not ablated (e.g., comparing generated samples with and without counterfactual context), the metric improvement cannot be attributed to the counterfactual mechanism.
all_issues_raised: IS and FID cannot verify semantic quality of counterfactual manipulations — a GAN can score well while generating counterfactuals indistinguishable from non-counterfactuals; no counterfactual-specific semantic metric is reported.

The 'rich prior knowledge sources' for counterfactual context conditioning are not specified; a narrow or low-entropy prior allows the generator to exploit predictability rather than learn diverse causal interventions.

The counterfactual conditioning is not ablated against unconditional generation; high IS/FID scores are achievable by ignoring the counterfactual context entirely, so the metric improvement cannot be attributed to the counterfactual mechanism.
all_issues_adjudicated: IS and FID cannot verify semantic quality of counterfactual manipulations — a GAN can score well while generating counterfactuals indistinguishable from non-counterfactuals; no counterfactual-specific semantic metric is reported.

The 'rich prior knowledge sources' for counterfactual context conditioning are not specified; a narrow or low-entropy prior allows the generator to exploit predictability rather than learn diverse causal interventions.

The counterfactual conditioning is not ablated against unconditional generation; high IS/FID scores are achievable by ignoring the counterfactual context entirely, so the metric improvement cannot be attributed to the counterfactual mechanism.
